_note: Slab c.500-390 BC — the PERSIAN WARS, the GOLDEN AGE OF ATHENS, the PELOPONNESIAN WAR, and the mid ROMAN REPUBLIC. All figures are historical (a few early-Republican Romans flagged semi-legendary per the tradition). Athenians & other Greeks use the existing GRK_ 'Classical Greece' house; Spartan royals stay in the existing MYC_ Spartan house so they attach cleanly to their parents; Romans use the existing ROM_ house. Roman dates are nominal placeholders on the schematic accession-year ladder the existing Roman slabs use; every child's year is kept strictly smaller than its parent's. CONNECTED TO EXISTING LINES: (1) PERICLES tied firmly into the ALCMAEONID family — a new node GRK_HIPPOCRATES_ALC (Hippocrates, brother of the existing reformer GRK_CLEISTHENES) is appended as a second child of the existing union GRK_MEGACLES x GRK_AGARISTE (Agariste of Sicyon); Hippocrates' daughter Agariste the younger (GRK_AGARISTE_Y) married Xanthippus and bore Pericles (Herodotus/Plutarch; Wikipedia 'Pericles','Alcmaeonidae'). (2) ALCIBIADES tied to the SAME Alcmaeonid line on his mother's side — Hippocrates' son Megacles (GRK_MEGACLES4) fathered Deinomache (GRK_DEINOMACHE), who married Cleinias and bore Alcibiades; Deinomache and Pericles' mother Agariste were siblings, so Pericles was Alcibiades' cousin-once-removed and (with Ariphron) his guardian after Cleinias fell at Coronea 447 BC (Plutarch 'Alcibiades'; Wikipedia 'Alcibiades'). (3) PAUSANIAS THE REGENT grafted onto the AGIAD royal house — Cleombrotus (MYC_CLEOMBROTUS) is appended as a full brother of the existing MYC_LEONIDAS1 (same union: existing MYC_ANAXANDRIDAS2 x his first wife MYC_ANAXANDRIDAS2_W2), and his son Pausanias, victor of Plataea 479 BC, follows (Herodotus 9.10; Wikipedia 'Pausanias the Regent'). (4) CIMON built as son of Miltiades, the victor of Marathon. (5) In Rome, CAESO QUINCTIUS appended as a son of the EXISTING ROM_CINCINNATUS (via his existing union ROM_CINCINNATUS x ROM_CINCINNATUS_W); his 461 BC trial & exile ruined his father (Livy 3.11-13). STANDALONE historical figures (drawn with a '—' placeholder spouse, no issue depicted — this is expected per the brief): Themistocles, Aristides the Just, the playwrights Aeschylus/Sophocles/Euripides/Aristophanes, the historians Herodotus & Thucydides, the sculptor Phidias, Socrates, the Spartan general Lysander, the Eurypontid king Archidamus II, the decemvir Appius Claudius, and Camillus. AVOID DOUBLE-PARENTING: every new person sits in exactly one child-union. Web-verified against Wikipedia 'Miltiades','Cimon','Themistocles','Aristides','Pausanias the Regent','Pericles','Xanthippus (father of Pericles)','Aspasia','Pericles the Younger','Alcmaeonidae','Alcibiades','Cleinias','Lysander','Archidamus II','Aeschylus','Sophocles','Euripides','Aristophanes','Herodotus','Thucydides','Phidias','Socrates','Caeso Quinctius','Appius Claudius Crassus','Marcus Furius Camillus'. No fabrication — attested pedigrees followed; unrecorded spouses drawn as '—' placeholders rather than invented.

families.note: THE PERSIAN WARS — ATHENS. Miltiades the Younger, the victor of Marathon (490 BC), x Hegesipyle (daughter of the Thracian king Olorus) > Cimon.

a.label:
Miltiades the Younger
c.550 BC · Athenian general; architect of the victory over Darius's Persians at Marathon, 490 BC · historical

b.label:
Hegesipyle
c.540 BC · daughter of the Thracian king Olorus; kin to the historian Thucydides · historical

children.label:
Cimon
c.510 BC · Athenian statesman & general; smashed the Persians at the Eurymedon (c.466 BC); leader of the conservative, pro-Spartan bloc · historical

families.note: THE PERSIAN WARS — ATHENS. Themistocles, the architect of the naval victory at Salamis (480 BC). Standalone.

a.label:
Themistocles
c.524 BC · Athenian statesman; built the fleet & engineered the decisive Greek victory over Xerxes at Salamis, 480 BC; later ostracised, he died in Persian service · historical

families.note: THE PERSIAN WARS — ATHENS. Aristides 'the Just', Themistocles' rival, hero of Marathon & Plataea. Standalone.

a.label:
Aristides 'the Just'
c.530 BC · Athenian statesman & general at Marathon & Plataea; a byword for fairness; founder of the Delian League's tribute assessment · historical

families.note: THE PERSIAN WARS — SPARTA, AGIAD graft. Cleombrotus appended as a FULL BROTHER of the existing Leonidas I: same union, the existing MYC_ANAXANDRIDAS2 x his first wife MYC_ANAXANDRIDAS2_W2. Cleombrotus commanded at the Isthmus in 480 & died soon after.

children.label:
👑 Cleombrotus
c.535 BC · son of Anaxandridas II; full brother of Leonidas I; Agiad prince who fortified the Isthmus against Xerxes, 480 BC · historical

families.note: THE PERSIAN WARS — SPARTA. Cleombrotus > Pausanias, regent for the boy-king Pleistarchus and the victorious commander at Plataea.

children.label:
Pausanias
c.510 BC · Agiad regent for Pleistarchus; led the Greeks to the war-ending victory over the Persians at Plataea, 479 BC; later suspected of Medism & starved to death by the Spartans · historical

families.note: THE ALCMAEONID GRAFT. Hippocrates appended as a second son of the EXISTING union GRK_MEGACLES x GRK_AGARISTE (Agariste of Sicyon) — brother of the reformer Cleisthenes, and grandfather of both Pericles and Alcibiades's mother.

children.label:
Hippocrates
c.560 BC · Alcmaeonid; brother of Cleisthenes the reformer; grandfather of Pericles & of Deinomache · historical

families.note: ALCMAEONID line. Hippocrates > his daughter Agariste the younger (mother of Pericles) and his son Megacles (father of Deinomache) — the two were siblings, the tie that made Pericles & Alcibiades kin.

children.label:
Agariste the younger
c.520 BC · Alcmaeonid; niece of Cleisthenes the reformer; wife of Xanthippus & mother of Pericles · historical

Megacles
c.520 BC · Alcmaeonid; son of Hippocrates; father of Deinomache, the mother of Alcibiades · historical

families.note: THE GOLDEN AGE — ATHENS. Xanthippus (victor of Mycale, 479 BC) x Agariste the younger the Alcmaeonid > PERICLES, the great statesman of the Athenian golden age.

a.label:
Xanthippus
c.525 BC · Athenian statesman & general; commanded at the victory of Mycale, 479 BC; father of Pericles · historical

children.label:
Pericles
c.495 BC · the pre-eminent statesman of the Athenian golden age; led the democracy for three decades, built the Parthenon, shaped the Delian League into empire · historical

families.note: THE GOLDEN AGE — ATHENS. Pericles x Aspasia of Miletus > Pericles the Younger.

b.label:
Aspasia
c.470 BC · of Miletus; partner of Pericles; renowned for her intellect & her salon in golden-age Athens · historical

children.label:
Pericles the Younger
c.440 BC · son of Pericles & Aspasia; Athenian general; among the six executed after the sea-battle of Arginusae, 406 BC · historical

families.note: THE GOLDEN AGE — the tragedians. Aeschylus, the father of tragedy & a Marathon veteran. Standalone.

a.label:
Aeschylus
c.525 BC · 'father of tragedy'; fought at Marathon; author of the Oresteia & The Persians · historical

families.note: THE GOLDEN AGE — the tragedians. Sophocles, master of the mature tragic form. Standalone.

a.label:
Sophocles
c.497 BC · Athenian tragedian; wrote Oedipus Rex, Antigone & Electra; added the third actor to the stage · historical

families.note: THE GOLDEN AGE — the tragedians. Euripides, the most modern & psychological of the three. Standalone.

a.label:
Euripides
c.480 BC · Athenian tragedian; Medea, The Bacchae & Trojan Women; the most searching & unconventional of the great three · historical

families.note: THE GOLDEN AGE — comedy. Aristophanes, the master of Old Comedy. Standalone.

a.label:
Aristophanes
c.446 BC · father of Old Comedy; Lysistrata, The Clouds, The Frogs; savage satirist of golden-age Athens · historical

families.note: THE GOLDEN AGE — history. Herodotus, the 'father of history', chronicler of the Persian Wars. Standalone.

a.label:
Herodotus
c.484 BC · of Halicarnassus; the 'father of history'; his Histories narrate the Greco-Persian Wars · historical

families.note: THE PELOPONNESIAN WAR — history. Thucydides, the analytical historian of the war itself. Standalone.

a.label:
Thucydides
c.460 BC · Athenian general & historian; his History of the Peloponnesian War founded critical, evidence-based historiography · historical

families.note: THE GOLDEN AGE — art. Phidias, sculptor of the Parthenon & overseer of Pericles' building programme. Standalone.

a.label:
Phidias
c.480 BC · Athenian sculptor; directed the Parthenon's sculpture & made the chryselephantine Zeus at Olympia & Athena Parthenos · historical

families.note: THE GOLDEN AGE — philosophy. Socrates, the fountainhead of Western philosophy. Standalone.

a.label:
Socrates
c.470 BC · Athenian philosopher; father of the Socratic method & of Western moral philosophy; condemned to drink hemlock, 399 BC · historical

families.note: THE PELOPONNESIAN WAR — ALCMAEONID graft, mother's side. Megacles (son of Hippocrates) > Deinomache, the Alcmaeonid mother of Alcibiades.

children.label:
Deinomache
c.480 BC · Alcmaeonid; daughter of Megacles; cousin of Pericles; mother of Alcibiades · historical

families.note: THE PELOPONNESIAN WAR — ATHENS. Cleinias x Deinomache the Alcmaeonid > ALCIBIADES; after Cleinias fell at Coronea (447 BC), Pericles & Ariphron became the boy's guardians — the tie to the Alcmaeonid line.

a.label:
Cleinias
c.480 BC · Athenian noble; fought at Artemisium in his own ship; father of Alcibiades; killed at the battle of Coronea, 447 BC · historical

children.label:
Alcibiades
c.450 BC · brilliant, mercurial Athenian general & statesman; ward of Pericles; his shifting allegiances (Athens, Sparta, Persia) shaped the Peloponnesian War · historical

families.note: THE PELOPONNESIAN WAR — SPARTA. Lysander, the admiral who crushed Athens at Aegospotami (405 BC) and took the city in 404, ending the war. Standalone (non-royal).

a.label:
Lysander
c.454 BC · Spartan admiral; destroyed the Athenian fleet at Aegospotami (405 BC) & captured Athens in 404, winning the Peloponnesian War for Sparta · historical

families.note: THE PELOPONNESIAN WAR — SPARTA. Archidamus II, the Eurypontid king who opened the war; its first phase (431-421) bears his name. Standalone (his Eurypontid branch, via Leotychidas II, is not otherwise in the tree).

a.label:
👑 Archidamus II
c.500 BC · Eurypontid king of Sparta (r. c.469-427 BC); led the invasions of Attica that opened the Peloponnesian War — its first decade is called the Archidamian War · historical

families.note: ROME — the mid Republic. Appius Claudius Crassus, the dominant decemvir; the board of ten codified Rome's first written law, the TWELVE TABLES (451-450 BC); his tyranny & the Verginia affair brought the decemvirate down. Standalone.

a.label:
Appius Claudius Crassus
c.495 BC · leading decemvir; the ten oversaw Rome's first written code, the Twelve Tables (451-450 BC); his tyranny & the Verginia scandal ended the decemvirate, 449 BC · historical

families.note: ROME — the mid Republic. Caeso appended as a son of the EXISTING Cincinnatus (his existing union). Caeso's violent resistance to the tribunes & his 461 BC trial forced his exile and helped drive Cincinnatus back to his plough.

children.label:
Caeso Quinctius
c.485 BC · patrician son of Cincinnatus; his fierce obstruction of the plebeian tribunes led to a famous trial (461 BC) & self-imposed exile, ruining his father · historical

families.note: ROME — the mid Republic. Marcus Furius Camillus, hailed the 'second founder of Rome': he took Etruscan Veii (396 BC) and, recalled from exile, rallied Rome after the Gauls sacked it (390 BC). Standalone.

a.label:
Marcus Furius Camillus
c.446 BC · Roman soldier & five-time dictator; captured Veii (396 BC) & restored Rome after the Gallic sack (390 BC); called the 'second founder of Rome' · historical
